xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AddressOfRegisteredOfficeOfCom: 13th Floor, Dr. Gopal Das Bhawan, 28 Barakhamba Road, Connaught Place, Delhi - 110001
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AddressOfCorporateOfficeOfComp: Unit No.101, Ground Floor, Tower-A, Signature Tower South City-1, Gurugram, Harayana-122001
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NotesGeneralDisclosureExplanat:
I. 13- The disclosures under this report are made on Consolidated Basis, i.e., for SGIL and its subsidiaries (together referred as ‘Signatureglobal (India) Limited’ or the ‘Group’).
III. 19- As of FY 2024–25, Signatureglobal (India) Limited has a total of 27 active project sites in Haryana — 22 under construction and 5 recently launched — spread across key urban clusters in the state. The Company’s operations are consolidated into two office locations, which also house its 13 subsidiary entities. The Group does not maintain any international offices or plants at present.
III. 20.A- All our construction sites are located across Haryana and Uttar Pradesh, with our corporate office based in Haryana.
IV. 21.A- The construction activities at our sites are outsourced. Hence, all the workers are employed by the contractors and vendors appointed by Signatureglobal (India) Limited. Approximately 16,850 workers at our construction sites were present as of 31 March 2025.
IV. 21.B- The construction activities at our sites are outsourced. Hence, all the workers are employed by the contractors and vendors appointed by Signatureglobal (India) Limited.
IV. 22-Includes four Whole Time Directors, who are also included in the Board of Directors.
IV. 23- The construction activities at our sites are outsourced. Hence, all the workers are employed by the contractors and vendors appointed by Signatureglobal (India) Limited.
VI 24. Yes, the provisions of Section 135 of the Companies Act, 2013, and the Companies (Corporate Social Responsibility Policy) Rules, 2014, apply to the Company. However, during the financial year 2024-25, the company was not required to spend towards CSR activities as per the applicable provisions.
VII. 26- The Group has established a comprehensive Grievance Redressal Mechanism through a range of policies and procedures. These include a well-defined Whistle-Blower Policy/Vigil Mechanism (https://www.signatureglobal.in/pdf/investors/Whistle-Blower-Policy.pdf), a Code of Conduct and Business Ethics (https://www.signatureglobal.in/pdf/investors/Code-ofconduct.pdf), a Grievance Redressal Policy (available on the Company’s Intranet), and a Policy for Prevention of Sexual Harassment (POSH) for all women employees (also available on the Company’s Intranet), enabling them to voice their concerns and grievances. Additionally, a dedicated Grievance Redress Committee (internally formed and governed) has been constituted to handle concerns raised by both internal and external stakeholders. The Group’s Secretarial Team manages grievances of the members of the Company through a dedicated portal—Online Dispute Resolution Mechanism for Members of the Group (https://www.signatureglobal.in/pdf/ONLINE-RESOLUTION-OF-DISPUTES.pdf)—and a specific email ID (investors@signatureglobal.in). Furthermore, the Group’s website features a “Customer Support” section (https://www.signatureglobal.in/customer_support.php), providing customers with multiple channels to lodge complaints or share feedback, including email, helpline number, SMS, WhatsApp, and a downloadable digital application.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescriptionOfBusinessActivity: Real Estate activities with owned or leased property
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ProductOrServiceSoldByTheEntit: Revenue from the sale of real estate properties
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ABriefOnTypesOfCustomersExplan: Signatureglobal (India) Limited, a leading real estate development company in India, is reshaping the housing market in northern India. Initially established as a key player in affordable and mid-housing, the company successfully transitioned to the premium housing segment in 2024, marked by the launch of two group housing projects with record sales value. The company now focuses on the mid-market and premium segments, emphasizing quality execution, value creation, reliability, and adherence to global standards.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheHoldingOrSubsidiaryAs: MAA Vaishno Net - Tech Private Limited

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: The company is exposed to regulatory risk arising from the evolving environmental, health, safety, and construction standards. Inadequate compliance monitoring may result in financial penalties, legal liabilities, and project execution delays.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Implementation of a structured compliance management framework, including dedicated tracking mechanisms, legal oversight, and periodic internal audits throughout the project lifecycle to ensure adherence to regulatory norms.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Increasing frequency and severity of climate-induced events such as floods and heatwaves pose significant risks to construction timelines, cost efficiency, and the structural durability of real estate assets of the company.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Integration of climate- resilient design principles, advanced stormwater management systems, and adaptive green building solutions to safeguard asset integrity and ensure long-term resilience against extreme weather events by the company.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Construction activities are a major source of CO₂ emissions, exposing the company to increasing regulatory and stakeholder pressure for transparent carbon disclosures and robust decarbonization strategies.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Deployment of low-carbon materials such as PPC cement, prioritization of local sourcing, adoption of energy-efficient technologies, and strict compliance with recognized green building certification frameworks to minimize embodied carbon and promote sustainable construction practices.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Enhancing energy efficiency presents a strategic opportunity to reduce long-term operational costs while improving the project’s attractiveness to environmentally conscious buyers and ESG-focused financiers.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Integration of rooftop solar solutions, solar-powered lighting, energy-efficient LED fixtures, and thermally insulating materials into project designs to enhance energy performance and reduce environmental impact, thereby aligning with evolving sustainability preferences.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Construction activities may contribute to habitat degradation and biodiversity loss, potentially impacting regulatory compliance and community perception. However, proactive preservation of green zones and integration of natural landscapes can enhance brand reputation, support regulatory alignment, and improve long-term asset value.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Ecological risks can be addressed through native plantations, integrated landscaping, and comprehensive environmental due diligence before construction, supporting biodiversity preservation and long-term asset value enhancement.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Inadequate engagement with local communities and regulatory bodies may lead to resistance, reputational challenges, and significant delays in project approvals and execution.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Establishment of dedicated stakeholder committees, regular community consultations, and structured grievance redressal mechanisms to foster transparency, trust, and timely resolution of concerns.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Workplace injuries expose operations to legal liabilities, ethical concerns, and potential disruptions, impacting both compliance and workforce morale.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Ensuring ISO 45001 compliance through on-site safety audits, consistent provision of personal protective equipment (PPE), and regular workforce training to uphold health and safety standards across construction sites.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Inefficient waste management can result in regulatory noncompliance, reputational harm, and environmental degradation. Conversely, effective recycling and waste reduction initiatives offer cost savings, enhance resource efficiency, and contribute to achieving sustainability certifications and green credits.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Implementation of construction and demolition (C&D) waste segregation protocols, collaboration with authorized recyclers, and reuse of inert materials to ensure regulatory compliance and promote circular construction practices.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Rising water stress in urban regions poses operational and regulatory risks for construction activities. At the same time, adopting water-efficient practices and recycling systems presents an opportunity to reduce dependency on freshwater sources, enhance resilience, and align with sustainability expectations.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Deployment of sewage treatment plants (STPs), rainwater harvesting systems, low-flow water fixtures, and dual plumbing infrastructure across projects to promote water conservation, recycling, and sustainable resource management.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Delivering positive customer experiences fosters brand loyalty, drives word-of-mouth referrals, and contributes to sustained value creation and competitive advantage.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Strengthening customer relationships through active CRM systems, structured post-handover support, and timely complaint resolution within defined service level agreements (SLAs), leading to improved satisfaction and brand trust.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Increasing reliance on digital platforms and technologies heightens exposure to cyber threats, data breaches, and operational disruptions, potentially impacting business continuity and stakeholder trust.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Implementation of robust information security measures, including ISO 27001 compliance, secure cloud infrastructure, and role-based access controls to safeguard digital assets and ensure data privacy.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Ethical misconduct or governance failures can erode investor confidence, damage corporate reputation, and result in regulatory scrutiny or penalties.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Adoption of a comprehensive Code of Conduct, mandatory director disclosures, and robust internal controls on related party transactions to uphold ethical standards and ensure governance transparency.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Building resilience to disruptions such as natural disasters and supply chain shocks presents an opportunity to strengthen business continuity, enhance stakeholder confidence, and create long-term value through proactive risk management.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Strengthening resilience through crisis management protocols, disaster preparedness planning, and digitization of critical operations to ensure business continuity and adaptive capacity during unforeseen disruptions.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Shortage of skilled talent can adversely impact project quality, delay delivery timelines, and increase operational inefficiencies, posing challenges to sustained growth and competitiveness.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Enhancing workforce retention and productivity through targeted upskilling initiatives, comprehensive health and wellness benefits, employee recognition programs, and strict adherence to POSH (Prevention of Sexual Harassment) compliance frameworks.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: As a labor-intensive sector, real estate is subject to heightened scrutiny regarding worker welfare practices. Gaps in compliance or inadequate safeguards can lead to reputational damage, legal liabilities, and project disruptions.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Promotion of ethical labor practices through contractor sensitization programs, accessible grievance redressal mechanisms, and strict enforcement of nondiscrimination and equal opportunity policies.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: High dependency on select vendors or unanticipated material shortages can lead to supply chain disruptions, resulting in cost escalations, project delays, and reduced operational efficiency.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Strengthening supply chain resilience through vendor diversification, identification of alternate sourcing channels, and periodic supplier performance and risk assessments.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Social resistance from local communities can delay or obstruct project execution, impacting timelines and costs. However, proactive engagement and targeted CSR initiatives foster community trust, enhance social license to operate, and build long-term goodwill.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Strategic CSR initiatives focused on education, healthcare, and community welfare, coupled with sustained stakeholder engagement, help foster trust, mitigate social risks, and enhance the social license to operate.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:RationaleForIdentifyingTheRisk: Adoption of sustainable building practices enhances market positioning by attracting environmentally conscious buyers and ESG-aligned lenders, while also delivering long-term operational cost savings.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:InCaseOfRiskApproachToAdaptOrM: Advancement of green construction credentials through EDGE certification, IGBC membership, integration of solar lighting solutions, and adoption of eco-friendly building materials to drive sustainability leadership and stakeholder trust.

All other internal policies are hosted on the Company’s Intranet and are not publicly accessible.

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:SpecificCommitmentsGoalsAndTar: Signatureglobal (India) Limited has adopted a three-phase ESG roadmap that assigns clear, time-bound targets and assigns ownership at the senior management and business-unit levels: Phase 1 – Foundation & Data Infrastructure (FY 2024-25). Publish our inaugural, GRI- and BRSR-aligned ESG report. Complete a Sustainable-Construction Blueprint covering low-carbon materials, passive-design principles, and wastesegregation standards. Roll out a digital data-automation system on every active project, ensuring monthly, audit-ready tracking of energy, water, and waste. Phase 2 – Target-Setting & Transparency (FY 2025-26). Finish an SBTi readiness assessment and submit near-term science-based targets for validation. Embed quantitative ESG KPIs—covering climate, HSE, labour standards, diversity, and responsible procurement—into annual business plans. Issue an annual ESG report to all stakeholders, including independent assurance of key environmental data sets. Phase 3 – ESG Leadership (FY2026-27). Commission priority carbon-reduction projects (onsite renewables, high-efficiency MEP systems, low-carbon concrete from the Group’s captive plant) that put the portfolio on a 1.5 °C path. Formalise a Green-Finance Framework and raise capital through green loans or bonds to fund further retrofits. Extend sustainability criteria across 100% of Tier-1 suppliers and contractors, with annual compliance audits. These phased commitments rest on ESG Steering Committee-(internal committee) approved policies for Net Zero, Climate Change, Energy, Biodiversity, Sustainable Procurement, Waste Management, and ESG Governance. Progress against each milestone will be reviewed quarterly by the ESG Steering Committee and shall be disclosed publicly in our annual ESG report, ensuring accountability and continual improvement.

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:PerformanceOfTheEntityAgainstT: At present, Signatureglobal (India) Limited is finalising the detailed, timebound ESG commitments outlined in our newly adopted three-phase roadmap (see preceding Point No. 5). Because these targets formally take effect from FY 2025–26 onward, no discrete, year-end performance metrics can yet be reported for FY 2024–25. During the year, management focused on the prerequisite groundwork, namely: 1. Governance & oversight – restructuring of an ESG Steering Committee, to be led by senior management, to guide strategy and implementation. 2. Baseline data capture – completion of the first group-wide GHG inventory and site-level water- and waste-data automation pilots. 3. Policy framework – ESG Steering Committee approval of the Net- Zero, Sustainable Procurement, Waste Management, and ESG Governance policies that underpin forthcoming targets. Until the full suite of quantitative KPIs (e.g., Scope 1-3 emission-reduction milestones, water-intensity benchmarks, supplier-audit thresholds) is formally adopted in FY 2025-26, performance can only be assessed qualitatively. Accordingly, no target shortfalls—or explanations thereof— are reported for the current period. The first comprehensive progress review, including measurable variances and corrective actions, will be disclosed in the FY 2025-26 ESG/BRSR report.

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:StatementByDirectorResponsible: As we begin implementing the ESG Vision and Roadmap approved this year, Signatureglobal (India) Limited has already laid the groundwork for transparent, accountable sustainability leadership: we completed our first double-materiality assessment, finalised a suite of foundational policies—from Net-Zero and Climate-Change to Waste-Management and Supplier Code of Conduct—and mapped them to GRI, SASB, and BRSR standards, giving every business unit clear guardrails for responsible growth. Our immediate priority for FY 2024-25 is the Year 1 “Foundation & Data Infrastructure” target set in the roadmap: publish a GRI/BRSRaligned ESG report, issue a sustainable-construction blueprint, and roll out project-level data ingestion automation for energy, water, and waste. Looking ahead, we view reducing embodied carbon in materials, aligning our decentralised supply chain with evolving policies, and strengthening data assurance as key opportunities to advance our ESG vision. With dedicated oversight from the ESG Steering Committee, tracking progress quarterly, we are committed to driving meaningful impact and look forward to sharing our journey in the first annual ESG report next year.

9- No, while the Company does not have a dedicated ESG Committee of the Board, ESG matters are overseen by an Internal ESG Steering Committee, which is responsible for formulating, implementing, and monitoring ESGrelated policies and performance across the organization. The ESG Steering Committee works in coordination with the Risk and Compliance Committee (sub-committee of Risk Management Committee) through regular updates and reporting. The key members of the committee and their respective areas of responsibility are as follows:
Mr. Sanjay Kumar Varshney: Head of ESG – Overall Strategy, Policy Direction & Monitoring Mr. Manish Swaroop: ESG Integration in Finance – Contribution, Impact & Business Implications Mr. Vidush Arya: Cross-functional ESG Integration – Nodal Coordination & Implementation Mr. Siddhartha Kapoor: Governance – Regulatory Compliance, Risk & Ethical Standards Mr. Vineet Kumar: Environment – Resource Efficiency, Emission Management & Reporting Mr. Ankit Singh: Social & Sustainability – Stakeholder Engagement & Social Performance

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:AntiCorruptionOrAntiBriberyPol: Yes, Signatureglobal (India) Limited remains steadfast in its commitment to conducting business with the highest levels of integrity, ethics, and moral responsibility. The Company will continue to strictly prohibit bribery and corruption in any form. All directors, employees, contractors, and temporary staff are governed by the Anti-Corruption and Bribery Policy, which is accessible on the Group’s Intranet. Regular training sessions on this policy will be conducted to ensure ongoing awareness and compliance. The Company’s Whistle Blower mechanism will remain a key channel for employees, directors, and third parties to report any suspected unethical practices, including those related to bribery and corruption, as per the Whistle Blower Policy/ Vigil Mechanism of the Company, for timely and appropriate corrective action.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionTa: Not Applicable, as there were no cases of corruption and conflict of interest which were reported during the year.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfTheEntityHaveProcesse: Yes, the Group has processes in place to avoid/manage conflicts of interest involving members of the Board of Directors and Senior Management. The Group has a detailed ‘Code of Conduct and Business Ethics’ for its Board of Directors and Senior Management, which includes detailed guidelines and mechanisms for avoiding conflicts of interest and for disclosing any such situations that may trigger a potential conflict. Further, an annual declaration is also received from the Board of Directors and Senior Management with respect to adherence to the same. The Policy is available on the Group’s website: https://www.signatureglobal.in/pdf/investors/Code-of-conduct.pdf

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheProcessesInPlaceToS: As a real estate developer, Signatureglobal (India) Limited operates in a sector characterized by long project lifecycles, where the reuse or recycling of completed developments typically falls outside the operational scope. However, structured waste management systems are in place to address waste generated during the operation and maintenance phases of buildings. These systems ensure materials are reused, recycled, or disposed of in full compliance with applicable regulatory requirements. Furthermore, non-performing assets such as obsolete or damaged hardware are systematically identified and sent to government-authorized e-waste dismantlers and recyclers, in adherence to the E-Waste (Management) Rules, 2016. All disposals are duly documented, with certificates of receipt obtained to ensure traceability and regulatory compliance.

Essential Indicator-
1- Going forward, Signatureglobal (India) Limited aims to explore and integrate specific technologies that enhance the environmental and social performance of its products and processes. The Company will continue to evaluate opportunities for R&D and capital investments in areas such as renewable energy, energy efficiency, and sustainable construction methods, with a focus on long-term value creation and positive environmental outcomes. As part of this effort, we will also strengthen internal mechanisms to track and report such investments in a more structured and transparent manner.
2.a- Yes. The Group adopted a Sustainable Procurement Policy, which outlines key principles for responsible sourcing across its operations. Implementation of comprehensive tracking and reporting mechanisms is currently underway, and data capture on procurement inputs will be initiated from next year onwards.
b- As mentioned above, we are now in the process of setting-up a comprehensive process to track and report such inputs and their quantities and will start the process of reporting these numbers from next year onwards.
4- EPR is not applicable to the Group’s activities.
Leadership Indicator-
1- No formal Life Cycle Assessments (LCAs) were undertaken in FY 2024–25. The Company is actively evaluating the feasibility of integrating LCAs into its future certified green building projects.
2- Not applicable, as the Group does not currently undertake Life Cycle Assessments (LCAs) for its products or services. However, we remain open to exploring LCA adoption in alignment with evolving sustainability standards and industry practices.
3- Signatureglobal (India) Limited is committed to advancing its circular economy initiatives by formalizing systems for tracking the reuse of materials such as aluminium formwork and construction and demolition (C&D) waste across its project sites. Going forward, the Group aims to move beyond qualitative assessments and implement structured mechanisms for material traceability and measurement. This will enable more accurate reporting and strengthen its approach to responsible resource utilization in the years ahead.
4- As the Group is engaged in the business of real estate development, the reclamation of products and packaging materials at the end of their lifecycle is not applicable.

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:WebLinkOfEqualOppertunityPolic: Yes, Signatureglobal (India) Limited has implemented an Equal Opportunity Policy in accordance with the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016. The policy is accessible to all employees via the Company’s intranet and is also embedded within the Code of Conduct for employees.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ThePremisesOrOfficesOfTheEntit: Currently, there are no differently abled employees on rolls; however, Signatureglobal (India) Limited remains committed to ensuring accessibility across its workplaces in accordance with the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016, to foster an inclusive work environment.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfMechanismAvailableToR: Yes, Signatureglobal (India) Limited is committed to providing a safe and supportive work environment for its employees. To facilitate the reporting of any suspected or actual misconduct, the organization has implemented the following mechanisms: Whistle Blower Policy/Vigil Mechanism POSH (Prevention of Sexual Harassment) Policy Grievance Redressal Policy Amber – a smart AI-based grievance redressal assistant that helps identify “People to Meet” cases Additionally, a dedicated Grievance Redressal Committee (internally formed and governed) is in place to address concerns raised by both internal stakeholders (employees) and external stakeholders (including contractors). Employees may also escalate their grievances to their respective Heads of Department (HODs) or the Chief Human Resources Officer (CHRO) as needed.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfOccupationalHealthAnd: Yes, Signatureglobal (India) Limited is certified under ISO 45001:2018 for its Occupational Health and Safety (OHS) Management System, which encompasses all its project sites and operational activities.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DesclosureOfTheProcessesUsedTo: The Group employs the following processes to routinely and proactively identify work-related hazards and assess risks- across both routine and non-routine activities—thereby reducing the likelihood of workplace injuries: Hazard Identification and Risk Assessment (HIRA): A structured HIRA process is in place to identify and evaluate workplace hazards by analyzing their probability, frequency, and severity. This process also assesses potential adverse outcomes, such as injuries or material losses. As part of the Group’s corporate safety strategy, comprehensive risk assessments are conducted for all activities — routine and non-routine across each section. For every significant risk identified, appropriate control measures are defined and implemented to mitigate potential harm. Daily Site Inspection Checklists: Project site teams conduct daily inspections using standardized checklists to ensure compliance with safety protocols and to promptly identify any potential hazards. Random Site Inspections: Cluster Safety Heads carry out unannounced site inspections. The unpredictability of these visits encourages continuous adherence to safety standards and fosters a culture of accountability and vigilance. External Audits: Periodic external Third-party audits are conducted as part of ongoing commitment towards ‘Zero Harm’, with the intent of continually enhancing of safety operations, benchmarking of best industry practices and maintaining of overall resilient safety posture at all construction sites. (A resilient safety posture means the Group has the ability to withstand and recover from disruptions or incidents while maintaining its core functions and safety standards). Qualitative & Quantitative Analysis of Safety Data: Safety MIS and other data are regularly analyzed to identify gaps, preventive & corrective action plans and identify opportunities to reduce safety violations/incidents and enhance safety culture amongst employees, laborers and the contractual workers. Investigations & Collective Learnings: Safety violations/incidents are investigated diligently, with focus on finding the root cause and then arriving at the best corrective & preventive action plans. The learnings are shared with team and other stakeholders as part of proactive hazard/risk identification and mitigation, collective learning & development.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMeasuresTakenByTheE: The Group places the highest priority on the health and safety of its employees, recognizing them as its most valued asset. All project sites are certified under the ISO 45001:2018 Occupational Health and Safety Management System, reflecting the Group’s commitment to maintaining safe and healthy workplaces. In addition to the initiatives outlined under Essential Indicator No. 10, the Group has implemented the following measures to ensure a safe and healthy working environment: Safety Training & Awareness: Regular toolbox talks, safety training sessions, and mock drills are conducted to enhance awareness and preparedness. Safety Observance Days: Events such as National Safety Day, Fire Service Day, World Environment Day, and Swachh Bharat Abhiyan are celebrated across all project sites to foster a culture of safety and sustainability. Engagement Activities: Quarterly “Safety with Fun” initiatives are organized to promote safety through interactive engagement. Personal Protective Equipment (PPE): Mandatory and job-specific PPE is provided and strictly enforced for all workers. Site Housekeeping: Cleanliness and orderliness are maintained through routine housekeeping at all sites. Checklists & Documentation: Standardized safety formats and checklists are used to ensure compliance with safe work practices. Critical Work Safety: Special focus is placed on high-risk activities such as work at heights (e.g. use of handrails, safety nets, and harnesses), electrical work, and material handling. Tools & Tackles: Proper tools and tackles are used, with a focus on operational integrity. Third-Party Inspections: All lifting tools and equipment undergo mandatory third-party inspections to ensure safety compliance. Risk Assessments: Comprehensive risk assessments are carried out for all site activities to identify and mitigate potential hazards. Fire Safety: Adequate fire-fighting equipment and water sources are available at each site. Emergency Preparedness: Each site is equipped with trained safety professionals, certified first aiders, and all necessary emergency equipment.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionTa: Management continues to foster a strong safety culture, grounded in shared values and proactive practices across all workplaces. No major safety incidents or significant concerns were reported during the year, reflecting our ongoing commitment to employee well-being. This culture is reinforced through systems such as work-permit controls, regular training, audits, and continuous monitoring. Gaps and learnings are promptly addressed with corrective actions and tracked to closure. In addition, the Group has introduced HappyMinds, a dedicated mental health initiative aimed at supporting employees’ emotional and psychological well-being.

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfMeasuresUndertakenByT: Our Contractors are required to submit valid PF and ESIC registration certificates, along with a workmen compensation policy, at the commencement of each contract. Additionally, copies of attendance sheets, wage registers, and payment challans are regularly obtained as proof of compliance with statutory obligations.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfAnyCorrectiveActionTa: Management visibly supports and promotes a strong safety culture, placing the highest importance on shared safety beliefs, values, and attitudes across the organization and at every workplace. During the year, no major safety-related incidents were reported, and assessments of health and safety practices and working conditions—both internal and across value chain partners—identified no significant risks or concerns. To ensure continued compliance and improvement, a monitoring mechanism is in place, including periodic audits of value chain partners by an external agency. Any observations are reviewed, and corrective actions, if required, are implemented and tracked.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheProcessesForIdentif: The Company identifies individuals or groups impacted by its activities and recognizes their significance in adding value to its business chain, both presently and in the future. Accordingly, key stakeholders identified by the Company include customers, investors and shareholders, regulatory authorities and government bodies, value chain partners (suppliers, service providers and contractors), communities and employees. Signatureglobal (India) Limited acknowledges the impact of its policies, decisions, products, services, and operations on these stakeholders. Aligned with its policies, practices, and procedures, it actively engages with its stakeholders to address differences in a just, fair, equitable, and consistent manner, and implements corrective actions when necessary. The Company also collaborates with relevant stakeholders to enhance sustainable and responsible business practices.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:ProvideTheProcessesForConsulta: Stakeholder consultations are conducted regularly by respective functional groups, business heads, and relevant company officers. At Signatureglobal (India) Limited, we practice continuous two-way communication to ensure stakeholder expectations are aligned with the management’s strategic direction. These engagements aim to identify and reassess material economic, environmental, and social topics relevant to our business. Insights gathered from these consultations are analyzed to prioritize key ESG issues. The proposed initiatives shall be presented to the senior management for their review and feedback, which is duly considered in defining the Company’s ESG targets and action plans.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfInstancesAsToHowTheIn: Yes, the Group, in consultation with stakeholders, has identified material environmental and social topics through a structured materiality assessment. This process helped determine the issues most relevant and applicable to the Group’s operations, on which appropriate actions are being taken. Furthermore, stakeholder inputs are actively incorporated into the development of the Group’s policies and processes to ensure alignment with their expectations and concerns.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfInstancesOfEngagement: The Group extends targeted support to vulnerable and marginalized stakeholders through its CSR initiatives. Regular engagement is conducted with local communities near project sites to understand their needs and challenges. Based on the feedback received, appropriate CSR initiatives are implemented to address those concerns. For further details, please refer to the CSR section of the Annual Report for FY 2024–25.

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheInternalMechanismsI: The Group recognizes the importance of timely and effective grievance resolution in upholding human rights and has established dedicated mechanisms through the following policies: • Whistle Blower Policy/Vigil Mechanism • POSH Policy • Grievance Redressal Policy A dedicated Grievance Redressal Committee (internally formed and governed) addresses concerns raised by both internal and external stakeholders. Additionally, employees may escalate grievances to their respective HODs or the CHRO, ensuring multiple accessible channels for resolution.

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:MechanismsToPreventAdverseCons: At Signatureglobal (India) Limited, we are committed to ensuring that complainants in cases of discrimination and harassment are fully protected against retaliation, punitive actions, or any adverse consequences for raising concerns in good faith. Our Grievance Redressal Policy, Whistle Blower Policy/Vigil Mechanism, and POSH Policy contain specific provisions that uphold the confidentiality of complainants. These policies safeguard individuals by ensuring that all reports, records, and information exchanged during the investigation process are treated as strictly confidential, with access limited to authorized personnel as deemed appropriate by the Company.

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfTheScopeAndCoverageOf: Signatureglobal (India) Limited routinely evaluates compliance with applicable human rights policies and regulations across its operations through internal reviews and, where necessary, third-party assessments. This proactive approach facilitates early identification of potential human rights risks or impacts, which are addressed through appropriate remedial actions and monitored to ensure effective resolution and continuous improvement.

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfCoverageAndImplementa: Signatureglobal (India) Limited’s existing and under development residential and commercial properties have implemented the mechanism for Zero Liquid Discharge, wherein the properties are provided with Sewage Treatment Plants (STP) with dual plumbing system. These systems are designed to ensure that all wastewater is treated and recycled and used for various needs such as toilet flushing, gardening, and landscape irrigation etc.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameOfTheExternalAgencyIfAnyIn: Yes, Laboratories approved and accredited by Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) and National Accreditation Board Limited (NABL) carried out an independent assessment.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfProjectRelatedToReduc: Signatureglobal (India) Limited is undertaking various measures to reduce GHG emissions across its operations. Signatureglobal (India) Limited is a pioneer of building Green Projects in Gurugram. Signatureglobal (India) Limited is an EDGE-certified Green Building Developer in its focused segment in Delhi NCR and is also a member of the IGBC, wherein it has also been recognized for its efforts toward sustainable building through awards such as the ‘Developer Leading the Green Affordable Housing Movement in India’ at the 8th IGBC Green Championship Awards, with most projects either EDGE or IGBC certified. The Group constantly incorporates sustainable best practices into the design and construction of its projects, wherein Signatureglobal (India) Limited has implemented environmentally friendly building concepts in many of its projects and aims to increase green cover in its developments to minimize net carbon impact. Sustainable practices include common area lighting with solar panels, LED lights, steel/aluminium powder-coated UPVC doors and windows, and high-performance glass that ensures better cooling and energy savings. Further, the Group uses environment-friendly and non-toxic materials to decrease CO2 emissions. In its endeavor towards sustainable development, SGIL has also signed MOU with Council on Energy, Environment and Water (CEEW) for Building an Air Quality Monitoring Network for Construction Sites.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DetailsOfWasteManagementPracti: Responsible waste management is a vital aspect of sustainable project development. In this regard, waste management encompasses eliminating waste where possible, minimizing it where feasible, and reusing materials that might otherwise become waste. Signatureglobal (India) Limited has adopted appropriate measures for the handling, storage, transportation, and disposal of waste across both construction and operational phases of its projects. These efforts form the foundation of the waste management practices implemented at our construction sites. For non-hazardous waste—such as metal scraps and construction debris—the maximum feasible amount is reused directly at the site. In addition, we work with authorized recyclers to ensure proper disposal and recycling of remaining materials. Hazardous waste is managed with care and disposed of through authorized third-party vendors, in line with applicable waste management regulations. This comprehensive approach of Reusing, Reducing, and Recycling not only upholds sustainability principles but also contributes to the advancement of a circular economy.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:NameAndBriefDetailsOfProject: Group Housing Colony project by M/s Signatureglobal (India) Ltd. Located at revenue estate of Village-Fazilpur Jharsa, Sector-71, Gurugram Manesar Urban Complex, Haryana

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DisclosureWebLinkOfEntityAtWhi: We at Signatureglobal (India) Limited have a Business Continuity and Disaster Management Plan, which is available on the Company’s Intranet. The purpose of having such Policy is to make sure that, in the event of an occurrence that might disrupt or endanger the Company, all business operations can be maintained at normal or nearly-normal performance levels.
xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DiscloseAnySignificantAdverseI: In the Current Financial Year 2024-25, the Group has not evaluated its value chain partners on the basis of environmental impact. However, the Group is in the process of developing a detailed evaluation checklist encompassing various parameters of environment, social and governance perspective to ascertain the compliance of its suppliers with the Group’s Supplier Code of Conduct. Accordingly, such assessment of the value chain partners will be done in the near future.

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMechanismsToReceive: As part of its Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) efforts, the Group undertakes various initiatives to support communities located near its project sites. To ensure effective resolution of concerns, a dedicated Grievance Redress Committee (internally formed and governed) oversees the Group’s Grievance Redress Mechanism (GRM). The GRC meets at regular intervals to review and update the GRM, respond to complaints, take appropriate action, and ensure that community grievances are addressed in a timely and transparent manner, thereby reinforcing the Group’s commitment to transparent, responsible, and inclusive stakeholder engagement.

xbrli:xbrl.in-capmkt:DescribeTheMechanismsInPlaceTo: To ensure sustained customer satisfaction and service excellence, a robust and well-defined mechanism is in place for the escalation and resolution of consumer queries and feedback. Each escalated case is tracked until resolution, with accountability teams ensuring that appropriate corrective measures are taken. Issues requiring specialized intervention or falling beyond the scope of routine handling are escalated to the relevant skilled department. This escalation follows a time-bound, hierarchy-driven protocol to prevent delays. The ‘Customer Support’ platform (https://www.signatureglobal.in/customer_support.php) provides customers with multiple mechanisms to lodge complaints or share feedback, including a dedicated email ID, helpline number, SMS, WhatsApp, and a digital application. All lodged complaints are monitored and responded to regularly within internally set target timelines.
